Technical Assistance 
(916) 322-5662 
Mr. Guy W. Kay 
Councilmember 
1660 Kearney 
St. Helena, CA 
94514 
Dear Mr. Kay: 
Administration 
•• 
Executive/legal 
•• 
Enforcement 
322.5660 
322-5901 
322-6441 
July 22, 1983 
Statements of Economic I nferest 
322-6444 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice, Our 
No. A-8 3-157 
Your letter requests our advice relative to the following 
circumstances: 
FACTS 
1. 
You are Vice President of Operations for Beringer 
Vineyards (Wine World, Inc.). 
2. 
Christian Brothers Winery (Mont La Salle Vineyards) is a 
competitor in the market place with Beringer. 
3. 
Christian Brothers has a facility (winery) near 
St. Helena which it desires to connect to the city's Municipal 
Sewer System along with a neighboring property owner, Richard E. 
Geyer. 
4. 
Together they have presented this issue to the 
St. Helena City Council as shown in the attached Exhibit "A". 
QUESTION 
You have asked whether you are disqualified from 
participating in this decision because of your position with 
Beringer and its marketplace relationship with Christian 
Brothers. 


Mr. Guy W. Kay 
July 22, 1983 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
Beringer is a financial interest to you because you receive 
income from itl/ and because you are an officer of it.~/ 
Therefore, as a public official you may not make or participate 
in making any decision which will have a reasonably foreseeable 
material financial effect upon Beringer. 
Section 87103. 
You 
have no financial interest in either Christian Brothers or 
Mr. Geyer or either of the subject properties. 
Consequently, 
the question is whether any impact of the decision on the sewer 
matter upon Christian Brothers will in turn have a significant 
effect upon Beringer. 
The Commission has adopted a regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code 
Section 18702 (copy enclosed) which elaborates on what 
constitutes a material financial effect. 
In the case of a 
business entity such a Beringer, the effect must be at least 
$1,000 and must also equal 1 percent of Beringer's annual gross 
income or 1/2 percent of its annual net income. 
However, if the 
effect on Beringer will exceed $50,000 net or $100,000 gross, 
then it is presumed to be significant. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18702(b) . 
If it is reasonably foreseeable that granting the request of 
Christian Brothers would result in a reduction of Beringer's 
annualized gross sales by $100,000 or more, then you must 
disqualify yourself.1.1 
If it woulc. reduce Beringer's 
annualized net income by $50,000 or more, then you must 
disqualify yourself.!/ 
If it is reasonably foreseeable that a 
decision denying Christian Brothers' request would enable 
Beringer to enhance its market position by $100,000 in 
!/ Government Code Section 87103(c). 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
specified. 
~/ Section 87103(d) . 
2/ This statement assumes that Beringer has annualized 
gross sales of at least $10,000,000; if it is less, then the 
1 percent test must be applied. 
!/ This statement assumes that Beringer has annualized 
net revenues of at least $10,000,000; if it is less, then the 
1/2 percent test must be applied. 


Mr. Guy W. Kay 
July 22, 1983 
Page 3 
annualized gross sales, again disqualification would be 
required. 
The same would be true if Beringer's annualized net 
would be increased by $50,000. 
Absent additional detailed facts, we cannot carry our 
analysis further. 
However, you are in the best position to 
assess the reasonably foreseeable effects upon Beringer from the 
decision affecting Christian Brothers. 
Should you have further 
questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5901. 
REL: km 
Sincerely, 
---:-7 
~! 
d<, I..--h.. 
/: 
~~_.~J,-:;. '-
]-(.. 
(,. 
,-
/ 
Ro bert E. Le i-di9h 
Counsel, Legal Division 
cc: 
St. Helena City Attorney 


Mr. Robert Leidigh 
Attorney 
State of California 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Mr. Leidigh: 
1660 Kearney 
St. Helena, CA. 
June 29, 1983 
94574 
Thank you for advising me on the initial actions available to 
me to prevent my acting in my capacity as a City Councilman 
in a manner which would cause me to incur a conflict of interest. 
You will recall from our conversation that the Christian Bros. 
Winery, also known as Mont La Salle Vineyards, and a property 
owner, Richard E. Geyer, are proposing to the City of St. Helena 
to install a sewer line which will serve the properties of each 
of them. 
When we discussed the matter, I had only fragmentary 
verbal information. 
Last night, June 28th, 1983, a document was presented to the 
City Council on behalf of the two proponents, and it is attached 
for your review. 
No action was taken by the City Council, and 
I specifically stated that I was reserving the right to discharge 
my responsibilities as a City Councilman, but a question had 
been raised concerning whether or not I would have a conflict 
of interest. 
I stated also that this potential conflict of 
interest was being reviewed by your office. 
The City Council 
agreed to delay hearing any detail until July 26th, 1983, to 
allow resolution of the conflict of interest question. 
The facts you should know to assist you in advising me are as 
follo\ll7s: 
1. 
I am Vice President of Operations for Beringer 
Vineyards. 
2. 
Mont La Salle Vineyards and Beringer may be 
considered as competitors in the market place. 
3 
The person who verbally questioned my possible 
conflict of interest is Richard E. Geyer, proponent 
of the sewer line 
... /2 


Mr. Robert Leidigh 
-
2 -
June 29 t 
1983 
This letter will have been reviewed by the City Attorney, 
Joseph Keebler, of Zeller, Keebler and Terry, Napa, California, 
telephone: 707-252-6633. 
I am certain that he is available 
for consultation concerning details from a legal viewpoint 
which I am unqualified to provide. 
In order to accommodate 
the proponents of the project, I would appreciate having 
your written opinion prior to our July 26, 1983, meeting. 
Very truly yours, 
Guy W. Kay 
GWK:em 
Attachment: 


June 24th, 1983 
H:::morable City Counc_ 
City of Saint Helena 
1480 Main Street 
Saint Helena, california 94574 
GentlerreI'} :

Further, recognizing the present limitations of 
local, State arrl Federal funding available for the construction of trunk 
ma.ins we hereby projX)se the following: 
I. 
!-t>nt La Salle V:L.'"1eyards and Richard E. Geyer will enter into 
a private agreement \\7h.erein M::mt La Salle Vineyards will provide 
a terrq:orary ease:rent arrl Richard E. Geyer \vill agree to construct 
at his expense an 8" Sanitary Sewer facility to serve their 
properties. 
II. Construction of the subject 8" Sanitary Sewer and appurtenances 
shall be perforrred to Plans and Specifications equal to or 
exceeding those of the City of Saint Helena current at the 
time of construction. 
III. The City of Saint Helena shall be entitled to revie\"r all plans for 
arrl inspect construction of said facilities, arrl the collection of 
such fees as are applicable thereto. 
IV. 
The City of Saint Helena shall, upon construction of said facili-
ties as described arove, and certification thereof being provided 
by City Staff, accept the subject 8" Sanitary Sewer t-1::lin and 
Appurtenances for operation and ma.intenance. 
V. 
At the time of acceptance of the facilities r-bnt La Salle Vine-
yards shall grant to the City of Saint Helena an ease:rent in 
perpetuity sufficient for the operation arrl ma.intenance of t.he 
facilities by City Staff. 
VI. 
U!:on connection to the facilities by r-bnt La Salle Vinevards and/or 
Richard E. Gever, their heirs and/oi:- assiqns, the City of Saint 
Helena shall be entitled to the collection of appropriate connection 
fees in effect at the time of connection or as otherwise agreed 
upon by the City and the undersigned. 


Honorable City Council 
.. June 24th, 1983 
Page 'J:\..D 
M:>nt Ia Salle Vineyards and Richard E. Geyer do hereby respectfully request 
consideration of the above by the City Council of the City of Saint Helena 
at its earliest convenience. 
o..rr representative will appear at your 
regular rreetinq of June 28th, 1983 to answer any questions which you rniqht 
deem appropriate. 
< 




Mr. Joseph Keebler 
Attorney 
Zeller, Keebler & Terry, Inc. 
929 Randolph 
Napa, CA. 94559 
Dear Mr. Keebler: 
1660 Kearney 
St. Helena, CA 
94574 
June 29, 1983 
Attached is a letter I intend to send to Mr. Leidigh of the 
FPPC. 
In the interests of time, I ask that you review it 
and, if satisfactory, to mail it in the envelope provided. 
If not, please call me at work (963-7115). 
All of this must 
occur before Friday of this week, as I will be leaving on 
vacation. 
Thank you for your help. 
Very truly yours, 
Guy I'J. Kay 
Attachment:

sections.question:
You have asked whether you are disqualified from 
participating in this decision because of your position with 
Beringer and its marketplace relationship with Christian 
Brothers. 


Mr. Guy W. Kay 
July 22, 1983
sections.conclusion: If it is reasonably foreseeable that granting the request of Christian Brothers would result in a reduction of Beringer's annualized gross sales by $100,000 or more, then you must disqualify yourself. If it would reduce Beringer's annualized net income by $50,000 or more, then you must disqualify yourself. If it is reasonably foreseeable that a decision denying Christian Brothers' request would enable Beringer to enhance its market position by $100,000 in annualized gross sales, again disqualification would be required. The same would be true if Beringer's annualized net would be increased by $50,000. Absent additional detailed facts, we cannot carry our analysis further.

1. 
You are Vice President of Operations for Beringer 
Vineyards (Wine World, Inc.). 
2. 
Christian Brothers Winery (Mont La Salle Vineyards) is a 
competitor in the market place with Beringer. 
3. 
Christian Brothers has a facility (winery) near 
St. Helena which it desires to connect to the city's Municipal 
Sewer System along with a neighboring property owner, Richard E. 
Geyer. 
4. 
Together they have presented this issue to the 
St. Helena City Council as shown in the attached Exhibit "A".

The Commission has adopted a regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code 
Section 18702 (copy enclosed) which elaborates on what 
constitutes a material financial effect. 
In the case of a 
business entity such a Beringer, the effect must be at least 
$1,000 and must also equal 1 percent of Beringer's annual gross 
income or 1/2 percent of its annual net income. 
However, if the 
effect on Beringer will exceed $50,000 net or $100,000 gross, 
then it is presumed to be significant. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section

If it is reasonably foreseeable that granting the request of 
Christian Brothers would result in a reduction of Beringer's 
annualized gross sales by $100,000 or more, then you must 
disqualify yourself.1.1 
If it woulc. reduce Beringer's 
annualized net income by $50,000 or more, then you must 
disqualify yourself.!/ 
If it is reasonably foreseeable that a 
decision denying Christian Brothers' request would enable 
Beringer to enhance its market position by $100,000 in 
!/ Government Code Section 87103(c). 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
specified. 
~/ Section 87103(d) . 
2/ This statement assumes that Beringer has annualized 
gross sales of at least $10,000,000; if it is less, then the 
1 percent test must be applied. 
!/ This statement assumes that Beringer has annualized 
net revenues of at least $10,000,000; if it is less, then the 
1/2 percent test must be applied. 


Mr. Guy W. Kay 
July 22, 1983  
annualized gross sales, again disqualification would be 
required. 
The same would be true if Beringer's annualized net 
would be increased by $50,000. 
Absent additional detailed facts, we cannot carry our

However, you are in the best position to

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city councilmember who is Vice President of Operations for Beringer Vineyards must disqualify themselves from voting on a sewer connection request by Christian Brothers Winery, a marketplace competitor.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Disqualification is required if the sewer decision would foreseeably reduce Beringer's net income by $50,000 or more or gross sales by $100,000 or more, or would increase Beringer's net income or gross sales by those thresholds if Christian Brothers' request is denied. The requestor must assess the reasonably foreseeable financial effects on Beringer to determine if disqualification is necessary.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request from Guy W. Kay (dated June 29, 1983) and the FPPC's formal advice letter response (dated July 22, 1983). The FPPC response clearly identifies QUESTION and ANALYSIS sections; conclusion is synthesized from the final analysis paragraphs as no formal CONCLUSION heading exists. Includes supporting documents: sewer proposal from Christian Brothers/Geyer and letter to City Attorney.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked whether you are disqualified from 
participating in this decision because of your position with 
Beringer and its marketplace relationship with Christian 
Brothers. 


Mr. Guy W. Kay 
July 22, 1983

CONCLUSION: If it is reasonably foreseeable that granting the request of Christian Brothers would result in a reduction of Beringer's annualized gross sales by $100,000 or more, then you must disqualify yourself. If it would reduce Beringer's annualized net income by $50,000 or more, then you must disqualify yourself. If it is reasonably foreseeable that a decision denying Christian Brothers' request would enable Beringer to enhance its market position by $100,000 in annualized gross sales, again disqualification would be required. The same would be true if Beringer's annualized net would be increased by $50,000. Absent additional detailed facts, we cannot carry our analysis further.

Mr. Guy W. Kay July 22, 1983
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter to a St. Helena city councilmember regarding potential conflict of interest arising from his position as VP of Operations at Beringer Vineyards while considering a sewer connection proposal from competitor Christian Brothers Winery, analyzing financial interest thresholds under Government Code Section 87103.
